directions.name: Tile-pair PTX state compaction around the paired export path
directions.hypothesis: The PTX branch has now won four rounds in a row, and round 4's paired export proved that aligning work to the two c_shared stages produces real runtime gains. The remaining hard guardrail is still 167 registers/thread and occupancy_limit_registers=1, not barrier or mio. The best final-round move is therefore a conservative PTX-local compaction step: keep the full 64x384 hot-band compute schedule and unchanged 64x96 tail, but reshape the hot-band state and helpers around six tile-pairs instead of one 12-tile accumulator object so the compiler can shorten lifetimes around the already-successful paired export path. This explicitly does not reopen the regressed half-panel compute split; it only matches persistent state to the pairwise export structure that already won.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Register pressure and live-range inflation around the 12-tile PTX helper surface are keeping occupancy at one block/SM even after control and export overhead were reduced.
directions.risk: This is the safest path, but it may only shave a few registers rather than crossing the occupancy threshold. It also risks perturbing the good codegen from the named-tile PTX path if the new pair abstraction is over-engineered.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the tile-pair compaction leaves registers and active warps effectively unchanged, because then the branch needs a more structural dataflow change rather than helper/lifetime cleanup.

directions.name: Full-width explicit PTX load-order follow-through without half-panel compute
directions.hypothesis: With barrier at 14.15 and mio at 2.32, the branch no longer looks primarily orchestration-bound. Long scoreboard is now the larger visible stall and compute-memory/DRAM throughput are already in the mid-50% range. A coherent next step inside the same 64x384 PTX branch is to push compute/load control further by replacing the remaining wrapper-style wmma.load/wmma.mma surface with a full-width explicit PTX dataflow, while explicitly avoiding the prechecked explicit mma.sync half-panel subpath that regressed. The point is to improve load order and fragment residency on the intact 12-tile hot band, not to split the tile into a separate 192-column compute path.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual load-to-use latency and fragment feed ordering inside the PTX compute body, now that barrier and mio overhead are largely under control.
directions.risk: This has more upside than dir_01 but is materially riskier in the last round. If the load-order rewrite drifts toward the old half-panel explicit mma.sync experiment, it can easily give back the steady progress from the last two rounds.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the implementation starts serializing the hot band into the already-rejected half-panel path, or if long-scoreboard and runtime do not improve together.

directions.name: Direct register-packed pair export beyond shared float scratch
directions.hypothesis: Round 4 already showed that pairing the export tiles was worthwhile, but the hot-band kernel still materializes each pair through shared float scratch before BF16 packing. A more aggressive follow-through on the same line is to keep the 64x384 PTX compute body and unchanged tail, but bypass more of the float c_shared round-trip by packing output from registers at the pair level and emitting global stores more directly. This keeps the direction squarely in the hot-band PTX branch and extends the export logic that just produced a real win.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Export-side shared/LSU work and short-scoreboard pressure, which remain visible even after the paired export cut a layer of the old path.
directions.risk: This is the highest-risk option for the final round because it changes export correctness, packing, and likely register behavior simultaneously. It may reduce shared work while still leaving the occupancy guardrail untouched.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the more direct export path increases registers or correctness risk without a clear reduction in shared/LSU pressure.

notes: All three directions remain strictly inside the 64x384 hot-band PTX microkernel branch and keep the 64x96 tail unchanged. Ranking is anchored on the new accepted base c9d030a: the PTX branch has now produced four consecutive real runtime improvements, and round 3 plus round 4 already knocked barrier and mio down to secondary concerns. The remaining guardrail is still one-block occupancy at 167 registers/thread with active warps stuck near 16.6. Because this is the final round, the recommendation favors a safer PTX-local state/lifetime compaction that builds directly on the successful paired export path, while still keeping one higher-upside full-width load-order direction and one more aggressive direct-export follow-through in reserve. Explicit mma.sync half-panel compute is intentionally excluded because that subpath has already shown negative evidence.
